headline: Your codebase as a knowledge graph
body: Codebase Memory MCP by DeusData turns any codebase into a persistent knowledge graph. Not a vector search. Not keyword matching. A structured graph where every function, class, and dependency is mapped to every other piece of code it touches.
image_keyword: code knowledge graph nodes dependencies visualization

headline: Searching vs understanding
body: Traditional code search tools return file paths and line numbers. Codebase Memory returns relationships. "This function is called by 14 other functions, modified 3 times in the last sprint, and has 2 known edge cases." That's the difference between searching and understanding.
image_keyword: code search vs understanding relationship graph

headline: 100K lines in 50ms. 1M lines in 200ms.
body: The indexing speed is what makes this practical for daily work. A 100,000-line codebase indexes in under 50ms. A million-line monolith takes about 200ms. No waiting. No background jobs. Ask a question about your code and get a graph-based answer instantly.

headline: 35% fewer AI coding bugs
body: AI coding assistants with Codebase Memory MCP write 35% fewer bugs on large codebases. The reason is direct. When your AI understands the dependency graph, it stops making changes that break unrelated modules. Context eliminates the most common class of AI coding errors.
image_keyword: AI coding bug reduction context awareness

headline: 2 minutes to full codebase awareness
body: Setup takes 2 minutes. Install the MCP server, point it at your repo, and your AI assistant gains persistent codebase awareness. No re-indexing on every prompt. The knowledge graph lives in memory and updates incrementally as your code changes.
image_keyword: MCP server setup codebase AI integration

headline: Follow me for 30 days of AI tools that make you faster, not just busier.
